raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "SB 457 is designed to prevent the misuse of the Builder’s Remedy while ensuring that cities in San Mateo and Santa Clara counties are treated fairly.",
    "The bill closes loopholes that allow speculative developers to bypass zoning laws.",
    "SB 457 provides strong incentives for cities to adopt compliant Housing Elements on time.",
    "The Builder’s Remedy was originally intended as a penalty for cities that fail to meet housing planning requirements.",
    "Some developers have exploited loopholes to file placeholder applications, securing entitlements without serious plans to build.",
    "Under current law, cities must wait for the California Department of Housing and Community Development (HCD) to approve their Housing Elements before being considered compliant.",
    "SB 457 ensures that a Housing Element is considered compliant from the date a city adopts it.",
    "SB 457 requires developers to submit complete, serious applications, preventing speculative filings that block land from being used for real housing.",
    "The bill allows cities to enforce their local Housing Elements once they have been properly adopted.",
    "SB 457 allows cities to maintain thoughtful planning and protect against rushed, haphazard development.",
    "The bill ensures that new development is integrated with public transit, climate goals, and essential infrastructure.",
    "SB 457 prevents speculative projects from straining already overburdened local resources.",
    "The bill aligns with AB 1886, ensuring accountability for cities that fail to meet housing obligations."
  ]
}
```
